name: nws-alerts-benchmark
description: Frozen benchmark for the live NWS-alerts memo domain (nws-alerts-tx). Three alert-episode prompt families share the feed scope; the flood episode is HELD OUT so its improvement can only come from lessons learned on the heat and storm episodes. Events are synthetic but shaped exactly like NwsAlertsSource output (the storm episode's metrics mirror a real 2026-07-18 poll: 22 active TX alerts, 21 severe). Do not edit after a baseline is recorded; bump `version` and re-baseline instead.

event.title: Benchmark: heat episode — 14 active alerts (2 extreme)
event.body:
Excessive Heat Warning: dangerous heat through Friday evening (South Central Texas)
Heat Advisory: heat index values to 108 (North Texas)

event.title: Benchmark: storm episode — 22 active alerts (21 severe)
event.body:
Severe Thunderstorm Warning: 60 mph gusts and quarter-size hail (Bexar County)
Severe Thunderstorm Watch: through 2 AM CDT (Hill Country)

event.title: Benchmark (held out): flood episode — 9 active alerts (7 severe)
event.body:
Flash Flood Warning: life-threatening flooding along the Guadalupe (Kerr County)
Flood Advisory: minor flooding of low-lying areas (Travis County)
